leadText: The MysticTicket (Japanese: しんぴのチケット Mysterious Ticket) is a Key Item introduced in Generation III.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] The MysticTicket allows the player to access Navel Rock , an island hidden among the Sevii Islands . There, the player can find, battle, and attempt to capture Ho-Oh and Lugia . Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen [ edit | edit source ] This ticket is accepted by the Seagallop . The player must have obtained the Rainbow Pass before using the MysticTicket. Pokémon Emerald [ edit | edit source ] This ticket is accepted by the S.S. Tidal only at Lilycove City Harbor. The player must have obtained the S.S. Ticket before using the MysticTicket.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description FR LG A ticket required to board the ship to Navel Rock . It glows with a mystic light. E A ticket required to board the ship to Navel Rock .

Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] Games Method FR LG ( NS ) Pallet Town (appears in the player's Bag after entering the Hall of Fame ) Distribution [ edit | edit source ] Distribution event announcement in Club Nintendo Main article: List of other event distributions in Generation III → MysticTicket Bulbanews Bulbanews has multiple articles related to this subject: San Diego Comic-Con attendees to get Mystic Ticket GBA ticket promotion announced The MysticTicket was distributed during various Pokémon-related events and occasions in Japan, the United States, and Mexico. In Japan, the MysticTicket was first distributed during various Pokémon Festa 2004 locations from July 17 to August 15, 2004. Its last known distribution was from December 27, 2006 to January 8, 2007. In the United States, the Ticket was first distributed at the Nintendo World Store on May 14, 2005. Its final distribution was during the Pokémon Rocks America 2005 tour, from September 17 to October 22, 2005. In Mexico, the Ticket was distributed only once during the Electronic Game Show , which took place from October 21 to 23, 2005. The distribution was a joint collaboration between Club Nintendo Mexico and Nintendo of America. In the Nintendo Switch re-release of Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen, the MysticTicket no longer requires an event distribution as in the original games. Instead, it automatically appears in the player's Bag once the player has become Champion and entered the Hall of Fame .
